content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Both features genuinely need loaders.py (mine: path_transform on BaseLoader/FileSystemLoader/PackageLoader/DictLoader/FunctionLoader/PrefixLoader/ChoiceLoader; yours: fallback_searchpath on FileSystemLoader). File-level disjointness isn't possible here. Proposal: within FileSystemLoader, you own the fallback/search-loop logic in get_source + list_templates; I'll only add a path_transform param to __init__ (extra kwarg, stored as self.path_transform) and one line at the top of get_source that rewrites  via the transform before your search loop runs - not touching your loop internals. I'll do the same lightweight pattern (constructor kwarg + one-line rewrite at top of get_source/get_loader) for the other loader classes. This should keep our hunks in different lines. OK to proceed on this basis?

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Finished path_transform. Added BaseLoader.__init__(path_transform=None) storing self.path_transform, and each subclass (FileSystemLoader, PackageLoader, DictLoader, FunctionLoader, PrefixLoader, ChoiceLoader) now takes path_transform as its LAST constructor kwarg and calls super().__init__(path_transform) first thing. In each get_source (and PrefixLoader.get_loader / ChoiceLoader.load), I save 'original = template' then rewrite 'template = self.path_transform(template)' if set, using 'original' in the final TemplateNotFound raise so error messages show the untransformed name. For FileSystemLoader.get_source my insertion is right after the docstring/signature and before 'pieces = split_template_path(template)', so it shouldn't touch your fallback_searchpath loop body itself - just be aware my param is appended after yours in __init__, and I also changed the final 'raise TemplateNotFound(template)' at the end of get_source to use 'original' instead - please double check that line doesn't collide with any fallback-related change you made there. All 841 tests pass on my end. Full patch written to patch.txt.
